prompt: Write a 150-word short story about a lighthouse keeper who discovers something unexpected in the fog. Do not use dialogue.
response:
The fog swallowed the sea whole, blurring the horizon into a bruised grey soup. Elias, salt-stained and silent, climbed the spiral steps of the lighthouse, the drumming of his boots the only sound. He lit the lamp, its beam cutting through the murk like a blade. Below, the rocks groaned under the weight of swollen waves. 

He checked the log, his fingers tracing years of ink—weather, ships passed, tides. A flicker in the beam caught his eye. Not a ship. A shape, deep in the fog. Not rock. Too smooth. Too… deliberate. 

He blinked. It was gone. 

Adjusting the lens, he swept the beam again. There, slowly emerging, a glass dome. Delicate. Ancient. Beneath it, a cluster of sea-dwelling creatures—bioluminescent, six-armed—tended to an intricate network of crystal filaments. They pulsed with silent light. 

Elias froze. He had kept this tower for thirty years. Never once seen this. Never believed. The fog thickened again. When it parted, the dome had vanished, leaving only the churning dark. 

He shut the lamp. The silence returned, but he no longer felt alone.
